symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden high-frequency, high-volume watery diarrhoea (>10 episodes / 24h)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute secretory watery diarrhoea of sudden onset is the defining feature.
Postural symptoms are a patient-reportable proxy for orthostatic hypotension and significant volume depletion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Extreme thirst with oliguria / dark urine, failing to keep up with losses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced urine output and unquenchable thirst indicate the kidneys are conserving fluid against ongoing depletion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe lower-abdominal crampy pain with tenesmus, no blood/mucus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colicky pain and urgency accompany enteritis; absence of blood/mucus argues against invasive/inflammatory colitis.
Lower abdominal pain must be cleared of a surgical cause by examination — not excludable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No recent travel, no sick contacts, no blood/mucus in stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of dysenteric features (blood/mucus) and no epidemiological exposure make an invasive/antibiotic-requiring pathogen less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic hypotension and resting tachycardia (BP 95/60 supine to 80/50 standing, HR 115)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective distributive/volume depletion — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers volume compromise from the postural symptoms, not from a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry mucous membranes and poor skin turgor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-assessed hydration signs; only partially inferable from video (dry lips) in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, diffusely tender abdomen with NO guarding, rebound or peritonism; hyperactive bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring exam that lowers surgical-abdomen probability — but requires the clinician's hands and is unavailable to the AI Doctor, which is why in-person assessment is mandated.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Stool MCS (+/- faecal calprotectin if symptoms persist)
investigation_details.expected_result: pending — indicated to characterise the pathogen if symptoms exceed 48h or dysenteric features emerge
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Acceptable to defer in acute self-limiting illness; escalate to testing if prolonged or bloody.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms/characterises an infective aetiology retrospectively; not available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute diarrhoeal illness with symptomatic volume depletion
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of high-volume watery diarrhoea plus postural symptoms, oliguria and orthostatic hypotension defines a dehydrated patient failing oral intake — the driver of the T4 acuity.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person clinical assessment with vital signs and abdominal examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Formal haemodynamic assessment and exclusion of a surgical abdomen cannot be done by chat and are needed the same day.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes, urea and creatinine (on in-person assessment)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify dehydration, electrolyte disturbance and acute kidney injury; guides IV rehydration.
If diarrhoea persists beyond 48h or turns bloody; not required for initial management.
